commands.description: Initialize a local Git repository in the current directory.
commands.mistake: Running git init in a folder that is already inside a Git repository, which creates nested .git files.

commands.description: Clone a remote repository to your local system.
commands.mistake: Forgetting to cd into the newly cloned directory after cloning, and running commands in the parent folder.

commands.description: Record staged snapshots in version history.
commands.example: git commit -m "feat: implement client-side regex tester"
commands.mistake: Writing generic commit messages like 'fix' or 'update' which make history hard to search.

commands.description: Create a new branch and switch to it immediately.
commands.mistake: Creating a branch from a dirty worktree (uncommitted modifications), which pulls local changes into the new branch.

commands.description: Merge target branch history into the active head branch.
commands.mistake: Merging without running git pull first, leading to conflicts on outdated commits.

commands.description: Discard all uncommitted changes and revert to a specific commit. Warning: destructive operation!
commands.mistake: Executing reset --hard when you have valuable uncommitted modifications; they will be permanently lost.

commands.description: Show a record of all commits, branch switches, and resets. Best tool to recover 'lost' commits.
commands.mistake: Assuming deleted branches or hard resets are unrecoverable without checking reflog first.
